verses.latin_vulgate: Aperi, Libane, portas tuas,  et comedat ignis cedros tuas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּתַ֥ח לְבָנ֖וֹן דְּלָתֶ֑יךָ וְתֹאכַ֥ל אֵ֖שׁ בַּאֲרָזֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Open thy gates, O Libanus, and let fire devour thy cedars.
verses.finnish_biblia: Avaa, Libanon, porttis, että tuli sinun sedripuus polttais.
verses.king_james: Open thy doors, O Lebanon, that the fire may devour thy cedars.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ulula, abies, quia cecidit cedrus,  quoniam magnifici vastati sunt :  ululate, quercus Basan,  quoniam succisus est saltus munitus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵילֵ֤ל בְּרוֹשׁ֙ כִּֽי־נָ֣פַל אֶ֔רֶז אֲשֶׁ֥ר אַדִּרִ֖ים שֻׁדָּ֑דוּ הֵילִ֙ילוּ֙ אַלּוֹנֵ֣י בָשָׁ֔ן כִּ֥י יָרַ֖ד יַ֥עַר (הבצור) [הַבָּצִֽיר]׃
verses.douay_rheims: Howl, thou fir tree, for the cedar is fallen, for the mighty are laid waste: howl, ye oaks of Basan, because the fenced forest is cut down.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ulvokaat, te hongat, sillä sedripuut ovat langenneet, ja ylimmäiset ovat hävitetyt. Ulvokaat, te Basanin tammet; sillä vahva metsä on hakattu pois.
verses.king_james: Howl, fir tree; for the cedar is fallen; because the mighty are spoiled: howl, O ye oaks of Bashan; for the forest of the vintage is come down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox ululatus pastorum,  quia vastata est magnificentia eorum :  vox rugitus leonum,  quoniam vastata est superbia Jordanis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֚וֹל יִֽלְלַ֣ת הָרֹעִ֔ים כִּ֥י שֻׁדְּדָ֖ה אַדַּרְתָּ֑ם ק֚וֹל שַׁאֲגַ֣ת כְּפִירִ֔ים כִּ֥י שֻׁדַּ֖ד גְּא֥וֹן הַיַּרְדֵּֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The voice of the howling of the shepherds, because their glory is laid waste: the voice of the roaring of the lions, because the pride of the Jordan is spoiled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Paimenet kuullaan ulvovan, sillä heidän väkevänsä ovat hävitetyt; jalopeurain penikkain kiljumus kuullaan, sillä Jordanin ylpeys on hävitetty.
verses.king_james: There is a voice of the howling of the shepherds; for their glory is spoiled: a voice of the roaring of young lions; for the pride of Jordan is spoiled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus Deus meus : Pasce pecora occisionis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֥ה אָמַ֖ר יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהָ֑י רְעֵ֖ה אֶת־צֹ֥אן הַהֲרֵגָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thus saith the Lord my God: Feed the flock of the slaughter,
verses.finnish_biblia: Näin sanoo Herra minun Jumalani: kaitse teuraslampaita;
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord my God; Feed the flock of the slaughter;

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ qui possederant occidebant, et non dolebant, et vendebant ea, dicentes : Benedictus Dominus ! divites facti sumus : et pastores eorum non parcebant eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֨ר קֹנֵיהֶ֤ן יַֽהַרְגֻן֙ וְלֹ֣א יֶאְשָׁ֔מוּ וּמֹכְרֵיהֶ֣ן יֹאמַ֔ר בָּר֥וּךְ יְהֹוָ֖ה וַאעְשִׁ֑ר וְרֹ֣עֵיהֶ֔ם לֹ֥א יַחְמ֖וֹל עֲלֵיהֶֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which they that possessed, slew, and repented not, and they sold them, saying: Blessed be the Lord, we are become rich: and their shepherds spared them not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä heidän omistajansa teurastavat niitä, ja ei luule siitä syntiä olevan; myyvät niitä, ja sanovat: kiitetty olkoon Herra, että minä olen rikastunut; ja ei heidän paimenensa heitä säästä.
verses.king_james: Whose possessors slay them, and hold themselves not guilty: and they that sell them say, Blessed be the Lord; for I am rich: and their own shepherds pity them not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego non parcam ultra super habitantes terram, dicit Dominus : ecce ego tradam homines, unumquemque in manu proximi sui, et in manu regis sui : et concident terram, et non eruam de manu eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֠י לֹ֣א אֶחְמ֥וֹל ע֛וֹד עַל־יֹשְׁבֵ֥י הָאָ֖רֶץ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְהִנֵּ֨ה אָנֹכִ֜י מַמְצִ֣יא אֶת־הָאָדָ֗ם אִ֤ישׁ בְּיַד־רֵעֵ֙הוּ֙ וּבְיַ֣ד מַלְכּ֔וֹ וְכִתְּתוּ֙ אֶת־הָאָ֔רֶץ וְלֹ֥א אַצִּ֖יל מִיָּדָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will no more spare the inhabitants of the land, saith the Lord: behold I will deliver the men, every one into his neighbour's hand, and into the hand of his king: and they shall destroy the land, and I will not deliver it out of their hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden en minä tahdo enää säästää maan asuvaisia, sanoo Herra; ja katso, minä jätän ihmisen jokaisen kumppaninsa käsiin, ja heitä kuninkainsa käsiin, että he maan musertaisivat, enkä tahdo heitä auttaa heidän käsistänsä.
verses.king_james: For I will no more pity the inhabitants of the land, saith the Lord: but, lo, I will deliver the men every one into his neighbour’s hand, and into the hand of his king: and they shall smite the land, and out of their hand I will not deliver them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pascam pecus occisionis propter hoc, o pauperes gregis ! et assumpsi mihi duas virgas : unam vocavi Decorem, et alteram vocavi Funiculum : et pavi gregem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָֽאֶרְעֶה֙ אֶת־צֹ֣אן הַהֲרֵגָ֔ה לָכֵ֖ן עֲנִיֵּ֣י הַצֹּ֑אן וָאֶקַּֽח־לִ֞י שְׁנֵ֣י מַקְל֗וֹת לְאַחַ֞ד קָרָ֤אתִֽי נֹ֙עַם֙ וּלְאַחַד֙ קָרָ֣אתִי חֹֽבְלִ֔ים וָֽאֶרְעֶ֖ה אֶת־הַצֹּֽאן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will feed the flock of slaughter for this, O ye poor of the flock. And I took unto me two rods, one I called Beauty, and the other I called a Cord, and I fed the flock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä kaitsen teuraslampaita, raadollisten lammasten tähden. Ja minä otin minulleni kaksi sauvaa: yhden minä kutsuin suloiseksi, mutta toisen sitovaiseksi, ja kaitsin lampaita.
verses.king_james: And I will feed the flock of slaughter, even you, O poor of the flock. And I took unto me two staves; the one I called Beauty, and the other I called Bands; and I fed the flock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et succidi tres pastores in mense uno, et contracta est anima mea in eis, siquidem et anima eorum variavit in me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאַכְחִ֛ד אֶת־שְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת הָרֹעִ֖ים בְּיֶ֣רַח אֶחָ֑ד וַתִּקְצַ֤ר נַפְשִׁי֙ בָּהֶ֔ם וְגַם־נַפְשָׁ֖ם בָּחֲלָ֥ה בִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I cut off three shepherds in one month, and my soul was straitened in their regard: for their soul also varied in my regard.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä hukutin kolme paimenta yhtenä kuukautena; sillä en minä voinut heitä kärsiä, eikä he minuakaan kärsineet.
verses.king_james: Three shepherds also I cut off in one month; and my soul lothed them, and their soul also abhorred me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi : Non pascam vos : quod moritur, moriatur, et quod succiditur, succidatur : et reliqui devorent unusquisque carnem proximi sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֹמַ֕ר לֹ֥א אֶרְעֶ֖ה אֶתְכֶ֑ם הַמֵּתָ֣ה תָמ֗וּת וְהַנִּכְחֶ֙דֶת֙ תִּכָּחֵ֔ד וְהַ֨נִּשְׁאָר֔וֹת תֹּאכַ֕לְנָה אִשָּׁ֖ה אֶת־בְּשַׂ֥ר רְעוּתָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I said: I will not feed you: that which dieth, let it die: and that which is cut off, let it be cut off: and let the rest devour every one the flesh of his neighbour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä sanoin: en minä tahdo teitä kaita; joka kuolee, se kuolkaan, joka nääntyy, se nääntyköön, ja jääneet syököön toinen toisensa lihan.
verses.king_james: Then said I, I will not feed you: that that dieth, let it die; and that that is to be cut off, let it be cut off; and let the rest eat every one the flesh of another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tuli virgam meam quæ vocabatur Decus, et abscidi eam, ut irritum facerem fœdus meum quod percussi cum omnibus populis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶקַּ֤ח אֶת־מַקְלִי֙ אֶת־נֹ֔עַם וָאֶגְדַּ֖ע אֹת֑וֹ לְהָפֵיר֙ אֶת־בְּרִיתִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר כָּרַ֖תִּי אֶת־כׇּל־הָעַמִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I took my rod that was called Beauty, and I cut it asunder to make void my covenant, which I had made with all people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä otin suloisen sauvani ja särjin sen, rikkoakseni minun liittoni, jonka minä kaikille kansoille tehnyt olin.
verses.king_james: And I took my staff, even Beauty, and cut it asunder, that I might break my covenant which I had made with all the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in irritum deductum est in die illa : et cognoverunt sic pauperes gregis, qui custodiunt mihi, quia verbum Domini est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֻּפַ֖ר בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֑וּא וַיֵּדְע֨וּ כֵ֜ן עֲנִיֵּ֤י הַצֹּאן֙ הַשֹּׁמְרִ֣ים אֹתִ֔י כִּ֥י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה הֽוּא׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And it was made void in that day: and so the poor of the flock that keep for me, understood that it is the word of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja se rikottiin sinä päivänä; ja raadolliset lampaat, jotka minun kanssani pitivät, tunsivat siitä, että se Herran sana oli.
verses.king_james: And it was broken in that day: and so the poor of the flock that waited upon me knew that it was the word of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi ad eos : Si bonum est in oculis vestris, afferte mercedem meam : et si non, quiescite. Et appenderunt mercedem meam triginta argenteos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֹמַ֣ר אֲלֵיהֶ֗ם אִם־ט֧וֹב בְּעֵינֵיכֶ֛ם הָב֥וּ שְׂכָרִ֖י וְאִם־לֹ֣א ׀ חֲדָ֑לוּ וַיִּשְׁקְל֥וּ אֶת־שְׂכָרִ֖י שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים כָּֽסֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I said to them: If it be good in your eyes, bring hither my wages: and if not, be quiet. And they weighed for my wages thirty pieces of silver.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä myös sanoin heille: jos teille niin kelpaa, niin tuokaat tänne niin paljon kuin minä maksan; jollei, niin sallikaat olla; ja he punnitsivat niin paljon kuin minä maksoin, kolmekymmentä hopiapenninkiä.
verses.king_james: And I said unto them, If ye think good, give me my price; and if not, forbear. So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus ad me : Projice illud ad statuarium, decorum pretium quo appretiatus sum ab eis. Et tuli triginta argenteos, et projeci illos in domum Domini, ad statuarium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יְהֹוָ֜ה אֵלַ֗י הַשְׁלִיכֵ֙הוּ֙ אֶל־הַיּוֹצֵ֔ר אֶ֣דֶר הַיְקָ֔ר אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָקַ֖רְתִּי מֵעֲלֵיהֶ֑ם וָֽאֶקְחָה֙ שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים הַכֶּ֔סֶף וָאַשְׁלִ֥יךְ אֹת֛וֹ בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־הַיּוֹצֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to me: Cast it to the statuary, a handsome price, that I was prized at by them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and I cast them into the house of the Lord to the statuary.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Herra minulle: heitä se pois, annettavaksi savenvalajalle; sitä suurta hintaa, johon minä heiltä arvattu olen! Ja minä oltin ne kolmekymmentä hopiapenninkiä ja heitin Herran huoneeseen, annettavaksi savenvalajalle.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto me, Cast it unto the potter: a goodly price that I was prised at of them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præcidi virgam meam secundam, quæ appellabatur Funiculus, ut dissolverem germanitatem inter Judam et Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָֽאֶגְדַּע֙ אֶת־מַקְלִ֣י הַשֵּׁנִ֔י אֵ֖ת הַחֹֽבְלִ֑ים לְהָפֵר֙ אֶת־הָאַֽחֲוָ֔ה בֵּ֥ין יְהוּדָ֖ה וּבֵ֥ין יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And I cut off my second rod that was called a Cord, that I might break the brotherhood between Juda and Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä särjin minun toisen sitovaisen sauvani, rikkoakseni veljeyden Juudan ja Israelin vaiheella.
verses.king_james: Then I cut asunder mine other staff, even Bands, that I might break the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus ad me : Adhuc sume tibi vasa pastoris stulti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלָ֑י ע֣וֹד קַח־לְךָ֔ כְּלִ֖י רֹעֶ֥ה אֱוִלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to me: Take to thee yet the instruments of a foolish shepherd.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi minulle: ota vielä itselles hullun paimenen aseet.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto me, Take unto thee yet the instruments of a foolish shepherd.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ecce ego suscitabo pastorem in terra, qui derelicta non visitabit, dispersum non quæret, et contritum non sanabit, et id quod stat non enutriet, et carnes pinguium comedet, et ungulas eorum dissolvet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י הִנֵּֽה־אָנֹכִי֩ מֵקִ֨ים רֹעֶ֜ה בָּאָ֗רֶץ הַנִּכְחָד֤וֹת לֹֽא־יִפְקֹד֙ הַנַּ֣עַר לֹֽא־יְבַקֵּ֔שׁ וְהַנִּשְׁבֶּ֖רֶת לֹ֣א יְרַפֵּ֑א הַנִּצָּבָה֙ לֹ֣א יְכַלְכֵּ֔ל וּבְשַׂ֤ר הַבְּרִיאָה֙ יֹאכַ֔ל וּפַרְסֵיהֶ֖ן יְפָרֵֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: For behold I will raise up a shepherd in the land, who shall not visit what is forsaken, nor seek what is scattered, nor heal what is broken, nor nourish that which standeth, and he shall eat the flesh of the fat ones, and break their hoofs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä katso, minä herätän paimenen maassa, joka ei etsi nääntyvää, ei holho nuorta heikkoa, ei paranna särjettyä, eikä tottele tervettä; vaan syö lihavain lihaa, ja repäisee heidän sorkkansa rikki.
verses.king_james: For, lo, I will raise up a shepherd in the land, which shall not visit those that be cut off, neither shall seek the young one, nor heal that that is broken, nor feed that that standeth still: but he shall eat the flesh of the fat, and tear their claws in pieces.

verses.latin_vulgate: O pastor, et idolum derelinquens gregem : gladius super brachium ejus, et super oculum dextrum ejus : brachium ejus ariditate siccabitur, et oculus dexter ejus tenebrescens obscurabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וֹי רֹעִ֤י הָאֱלִיל֙ עֹזְבִ֣י הַצֹּ֔אן חֶ֥רֶב עַל־זְרוֹע֖וֹ וְעַל־עֵ֣ין יְמִינ֑וֹ זְרֹעוֹ֙ יָב֣וֹשׁ תִּיבָ֔שׁ וְעֵ֥ין יְמִינ֖וֹ כָּהֹ֥ה תִכְהֶֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: O shepherd, and idol, that forsaketh the flock: the sword upon his arm and upon his right eye: his arm shall quite wither away, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened.
verses.finnish_biblia: Voi sitä kelvotointa paimenta, joka lauman hylkää! tulkaan miekka hänen käsivarteensa ja oikiaan silmään; kuivukoon hänen käsivartensa peräti, ja hänen oikea silmänsä tulkoon kokonansa pimiäksi!
verses.king_james: Woe to the idol shepherd that leaveth the flock! the sword shall be upon his arm, and upon his right eye: his arm shall be clean dried up, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened.
